# Development Station New Chat Handoff

Date: 2026-05-26
Project: Development Station Power Supply
Phase: Rev-B HAT — STM32 supporting circuit

## Current Objective

Add the STM32 supporting cast to the Rev-B HAT schematic: decoupling capacitors on U10 power pins,
BOOT0 pull-down strap, NRST RC/filter network, and a programming header. Then fix the
verify-connector-contract.ps1 script which references old net names from the completed U10 migration.

## Why This Session Was Stopped

U10 (STM32 Blue Pill) 44-pin migration is fully complete. ERC is 0 errors / 9 warnings (all
environmental). User requested a clean stop before starting the supporting circuit work.

## Stop Point Summary — What Was Finished

1. All 44 U10 pins wired or explicitly NC'd.
2. AMS1117-3.3 (U9) added as on-board 3.3V LDO (VI→+5V_Boot, VO→+3.3V Boot, GND).
3. CTS/RTS wired: PA11→CTS→R77→J13 pin 6; PA12→RTS→R73→J13 pin 4.
4. Development GPIO connector pins assigned: PB5→J12 pin 6, PB6→J12 pin 8, PB7→R86→J13 pin 10.
5. Legacy net names cleaned up: 3V3_ESP→+3.3V Boot, GPIO10/11/12→PB5/PB6/PB7.
6. stm32_BLUE custom symbol GND pin types fixed — all pin-type ERC errors eliminated.
7. docs/U10_WIRING_WORKTHROUGH.md updated to final completed state.

## ERC State At Stop

- Errors: 0
- Warnings: 9 (all environmental — missing library paths and dangling stub global labels for UART1_TX/RX/NRST)
- No electrical wiring issues remain.

## Known Failing Check

verify-connector-contract.ps1 exits code 1. Likely references old net names that were renamed this session.
Fix this early next session before starting schematic work.

## Files That Matter

- hardware/kicad/dsp-regulator-hat-rev-b/DSP-Regulator-HAT-RevB.kicad_sch — active schematic
- hardware/kicad/dsp-regulator-hat-rev-b/DSP-Regulator-HAT-RevB.net — last exported netlist
- docs/U10_WIRING_WORKTHROUGH.md — U10 pin assignment reference (complete)
- docs/STM32_BLUEPILL_PIN_TABLE.md — validation log (needs one entry added)
- scripts/verify-connector-contract.ps1 — needs net-name update

## Next Session Actions (In Order)

1. Fix verify-connector-contract.ps1 net references.
2. Add decoupling caps on U10 VDD pins (100nF each on 5V pin 18 and 3.3V pins 20/38).
3. Add BOOT0 pull-down strap (10kΩ to GND, with optional jumper/test point for override).
4. Add NRST RC network (100nF cap to GND + optional 10kΩ pull-up to 3.3V).
5. Add programming header — SWD 4-pin (VCC/GND/SWDIO/SWDCLK) or UART 1x4 (VCC/GND/TX/RX).
6. Re-export netlist, re-run ERC, update STM32_BLUEPILL_PIN_TABLE.md.

## Critical Open Risk

U9 symbol migration metadata and surrounding net topology are not yet fully pin-reconciled.
There is a real risk of semantic mismatch between intended Blue Pill pin function and actual connected net by coordinate/pin number.

This is why final BOOT0 strap and final NRST RC implementation were intentionally deferred.

## Known Verification State

Strict baseline verifier remains failed (expected in this project context):
- powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/verify-connector-contract.ps1 -RegulatorNetlist "hardware/kicad/dsp-regulator-rev-b/DSP-Regulator-RevB.net" -HatNetlist "hardware/kicad/dsp-regulator-hat-rev-b/DSP-Regulator-HAT-RevB.net" -Mode baseline -BaselineFeedbackPolicy strict
- last recorded exit code: 1

Do not treat this as a new migration regression until netlists are re-exported post-reconciliation.

## Rules For Next Session

1. Stay focused on Rev-B HAT U9 migration closure.
2. Do not widen into unrelated redesign/planning topics.
3. Do not run bench work unless explicitly requested.
4. Use conservative edits and validate after each migration step.
5. Keep handoff docs synchronized as soon as major migration milestones are reached.

## Priority Order Next Session

1. Build explicit U9 pin-number to project-net mapping table from current schematic state.
2. Reconcile mismatched pin ownership in schematic wiring and labels.
3. Implement final BOOT0 default-low strap and deterministic NRST hardware behavior.
4. Re-export HAT netlist and run verification checks to capture post-change deltas.
5. Update docs and close/retain migration blockers with evidence.

## First Action Next Session

Open and compare, in order:
1. HANDOFF.md
2. docs/STM32_BLUEPILL_PIN_TABLE.md
3. hardware/kicad/dsp-regulator-hat-rev-b/DSP-Regulator-HAT-RevB.kicad_sch

Then perform U9 pin-by-pin reconciliation before any new functional hardware additions.

## Suggested Prompt For New Chat

Continue Rev-B HAT U9 migration closure from HANDOFF.md and NEW_CHAT_HANDOFF.rmd. First do explicit U9 pin-by-pin reconciliation in DSP-Regulator-HAT-RevB.kicad_sch, then implement final BOOT0 default strap and NRST deterministic reset network, then re-export netlist and run connector verification checks. Keep scope tightly on migration closure only.
